title: RAF Typhoon jet shoots down Iranian drone heading to Qatar

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: A RAF Typhoon jet has shot down an Iranian drone heading toward Qatari airspace.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: This article is brought to you by our exclusive subscriber partnership with our sister title USA Today, and has been written by our American colleagues. It does not necessarily reflect the view of The Herald. On Sunday evening, a Ministry of Defence spokesperson confirmed the incident had taken place during a “defensive air patrol”.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: They said: “On 1 March 2026, a Royal Air Force Typhoon operating from Qatar as part of the joint UK–Qatar Typhoon Squadron, successfully took out an Iranian drone heading towards Qatari territory. “The Typhoon jet was conducting a defensive air patrol and used an air‑to‑air missile to shoot down the drone, ensuring the security of Qatar’s airspace and British interests in the region. Our Armed Forces are playing a vital role to protect our people, our interests and our Allies.”

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – RAF Typhoon shoots down Iranian drone heading towards Qatari airspace
